title: Unify DI and Composition Roots across 4 Platforms
note: Follow-on from ARCH-AUDIT-002. Audit output: docs/architecture/DI-Deviation-Matrix.md, docs/architecture/DI-Deviation-Diagram.md.
description: Unify DI composition across 5 hosts (Desktop, Android, Web, Director, VS Extension) into a shared AddMcpHost registration contract, eliminating 12 provider-construction/composition sites in app code, 3 post-build Dispatcher hacks, 2 duplicate registration methods, 2 container leaks, and 305 lines of wrapper/factory code.

Each implementation task (IT-01 through IT-24) specifies exact source files, exact line numbers, exact current code patterns, and exact target code patterns. Technical details (TD1-TD26) provide the change-point catalog organized by phase.

New shared contract lives in src/McpServer.UI.Core/Hosting/ and must support two host modes: command-target hosts (Desktop, Android, Web, Director) and non-command-target hosts (VS Extension Todo host) plus early connection bootstrap for Avalonia before later auth/context unification phases.

Key design decisions: (1) logging refactor requires logger factories that actually honor AddProvider before AddCqrsLoggerProvider can replace post-build hacks; (2) McpHostLifetimeStrategy controls Singleton vs Scoped Dispatcher; (3) IHostIdentityProvider standardizes auth later, but Phase 1 must already carry enough connection bootstrap data for Desktop/Android migration.

Migration remains incremental (no flag day): Phase 0 validates + adds tests, Phase 1 adds shared contract + compat shim, Phases 2-5 migrate hosts one at a time, Phase 6 converges, Phase 7 deletes obsolete code.
technicalDetails: TD1 INVENTORY — 12 provider-construction/composition sites in src/: 7 direct BuildServiceProvider invocations at (1) src/McpServerManager.Core/Services/UiCoreAppRuntime.cs L45, (2) src/McpServerManager.Core/Services/UiCoreServiceProviderFactory.cs L47, (3) src/McpServer.UI.Core/Services/UiCoreServiceProviderFactory.cs L81, (4) src/McpServerManager.Core/Services/ChatWindowViewModelFactory.cs L61, (5) src/McpServerManager.Core/Services/LocalCqrsDispatcher.cs L29, (6) src/McpServer.VsExtension.McpTodo.Vsix/McpServerMcpTodoToolWindowPane.cs L42, (7) src/McpServer.Director/DirectorServiceRegistration.cs L90 inside BuildAndFinalize(). Plus 5 wrapper/provider-entry sites that delegate to BuildAndFinalize: (8) src/McpServer.Director/Program.cs L47-50 BuildServiceProvider(), (9) src/McpServer.Director/Commands/InteractiveCommand.cs L37-39, (10) src/McpServer.Director/Commands/CommandHelpers.cs L51-56, (11) src/McpServer.Director/Commands/AuthCommands.cs L75-79, (12) src/McpServer.Director/Commands/AgentHostCommand.cs L90-118.

TD2 INVENTORY — 3 post-build Dispatcher-as-ILoggerProvider hack sites. All execute: sp.GetRequiredService<ILoggerFactory>().AddProvider(sp.GetRequiredService<Dispatcher>()). Sites: (1) src/McpServerManager.Core/Services/UiCoreAppRuntime.cs L46-47, (2) src/McpServer.UI.Core/Services/UiCoreServiceProviderFactory.cs L82-83, (3) src/McpServer.Director/DirectorServiceRegistration.cs L94-95. Web explicitly avoids this at src/McpServer.Web/WebServiceRegistration.cs L29-32 because scoped Dispatcher cannot be registered as a singleton logger provider.

TD3 INVENTORY — 2 duplicate registration methods with overlapping service sets: (A) src/McpServerManager.Core/ServiceCollectionExtensions.cs L43-130 AddMcpServerManagerUiCore — 14 nullable params, Avalonia-specific adapters at L94-96, IHealthApiClient at L88, ICommandTarget + 11 sub-interface registrations at L98-110. (B) src/McpServer.UI.Core/Services/UiCoreServiceProviderFactory.cs L12-93 Build — 12 nullable params, NoOp fallback defaults at L54-59, ICommandTarget + 9 sub-interfaces at L62-72. Both register AppLogService logging, conditional API adapters, infrastructure services, AddCqrsDispatcher, AddCqrsHandlers, and AddUiCore.

TD4 INVENTORY — 2 container leak sites: (1) src/McpServerManager.Core/Services/ChatWindowViewModelFactory.cs L57-61 builds a provider, resolves Dispatcher, and drops the provider reference without disposal. (2) src/McpServerManager.Core/Services/LocalCqrsDispatcher.cs L9-29 keeps a static Lazy<ServiceProvider> for process lifetime and never disposes it.

TD5 INVENTORY — 5 host composition roots with exact entry points: (1) Desktop: src/McpServerManager.Desktop/App.axaml.cs OnFrameworkInitializationCompleted L39+ constructs ConnectionViewModel and MainWindowViewModel manually. (2) Android: src/McpServerManager.Android/App.axaml.cs OnFrameworkInitializationCompleted L43+ does the same. (3) Web: src/McpServer.Web/WebServiceRegistration.cs L13-66 builds the Blazor scoped graph. (4) Director: src/McpServer.Director/DirectorServiceRegistration.cs L31-106 plus src/McpServer.Director/Program.cs L47-50 top-level provider wrapper. (5) VS Extension: src/McpServer.VsExtension.McpTodo.Vsix/McpServerMcpTodoToolWindowPane.cs constructor L15-48.

TD6 PHASE 1 NEW FILE — src/McpServer.UI.Core/Hosting/McpHostOptions.cs: Options class replacing nullable optional-service signatures. CommandTarget must be OPTIONAL, not required, because VSIX todo hosting uses Dispatcher directly from IServiceProvider and does not implement ICommandTarget. Required properties by scenario: (A) common: McpHostLifetimeStrategy Lifetime, WorkspaceContextViewModel? WorkspaceContext, Assembly[]? AdditionalHandlerAssemblies; (B) explicit service mode: ITodoApiClient? TodoClient, IWorkspaceApiClient? WorkspaceClient, IVoiceApiClient? VoiceClient, ISessionLogApiClient? SessionLogClient, IEventStreamApiClient? EventStreamClient, IHealthApiClient? HealthClient; (C) connection-bootstrap mode for Desktop/Android migration: Uri? McpBaseUrl, string? ApiKey, string? BearerToken, Func<string?>? ResolveWorkspacePath, Func<McpServerClient>? ClientFactoryOverride, Func<McpServerClient>? PromptClientFactoryOverride. Also carry infrastructure overrides: IFileSystemService, IProcessLauncherService, ITimerService, IJsonParsingService, IFileSystemWatcherService, IClipboardService, IUiDispatcherService, IConnectionAuthService, ISpeechFilterService.

TD7 PHASE 1 NEW FILE — src/McpServer.UI.Core/Hosting/McpHostLifetimeStrategy.cs: public enum McpHostLifetimeStrategy { Singleton, Scoped }. Singleton hosts (Desktop, Android, Director, most VSIX services) get AddCqrsDispatcher(); scoped hosts (Web per request) get AddScoped<Dispatcher>() + AddScoped<IDispatcher>(...). The shared contract must not force scoped-host code to emulate singleton logging or singleton hosts to emulate scopes.

TD8 PHASE 1 NEW FILE — src/McpServer.UI.Core/Hosting/McpHostBuilderExtensions.cs: public static IServiceCollection AddMcpHost(this IServiceCollection services, Action<McpHostOptions> configure). Body: (1) materialize options, (2) register logging infrastructure, (3) if explicit API clients were supplied register them; else if connection-bootstrap inputs were supplied create McpServerClient / prompt client and then register McpTodoService, McpWorkspaceService, McpSessionLogService, McpVoiceConversationService, McpAgentEventStreamService plus their API adapters, (4) register infrastructure services using provided-or-default values, (5) register ICommandTarget and granular sub-interfaces only when options.CommandTarget is not null, (6) if Lifetime=Singleton call AddCqrsDispatcher(); if Lifetime=Scoped register Dispatcher/IDispatcher as scoped, (7) call AddCqrsHandlers for UI.Core + AdditionalHandlerAssemblies, (8) call AddUiCore(additionalAssemblies), (9) register WorkspaceContext if provided. AddCqrsLoggerProvider is allowed only after the logger factory work in TD16 is complete.

TD9 PHASE 1 NO CHANGE — src/McpServer.UI.Core/ServiceCollectionExtensions.cs AddUiCore L24-88 remains the leaf registration API. It already encapsulates TryAdd fallbacks (IRoleContext/IAuthorizationPolicyService/IClipboardService/IAppLogService/ISpeechFilterService/IUiDispatcherService/IConnectionAuthService/IChatWindowService/IVoiceConversationService/ITimerService/IHealthApiClient), singleton WorkspaceContextViewModel, singleton WorkspaceAutoSelector, singleton BackendConnectionMonitor, and transient ViewModels. AddMcpHost should build on this rather than replace it.

TD10 PHASE 1 MODIFY — src/McpServerManager.Core/ServiceCollectionExtensions.cs L43-130 AddMcpServerManagerUiCore becomes an [Obsolete] compatibility shim. Replace the 80+ line manual registration body with delegation to services.AddMcpHost(options => { map existing todo/workspace/voice/session/event/health parameters; set Lifetime = Singleton; set CommandTarget = commandTarget; set Avalonia adapters for UiDispatcher, SpeechFilter, ConnectionAuth; forward additional handler assemblies }). Preserve current public signature until Phase 7 deletion.

TD11 PHASE 2A MODIFY — src/McpServerManager.Desktop/App.axaml.cs OnFrameworkInitializationCompleted. CURRENT: builds AvaloniaUiDispatcherService, UiDispatcherHost.Configure, ConnectionViewModel manually, then new MainWindowViewModel(clipboardService, mcpBaseUrl, mcpApiKey, bearerToken, notificationService, uiDispatcher) at L92. TARGET: one root ServiceCollection configured with AddMcpHost(connection-bootstrap mode, Lifetime=Singleton, CommandTarget supplied later for MainWindowViewModel) plus Desktop-specific adapters. App lifetime owns the ServiceProvider and resolves ConnectionViewModel and MainWindowViewModel from DI.

TD12 PHASE 2B MODIFY — src/McpServerManager.Android/App.axaml.cs mirrors TD11. CURRENT: constructs ConnectionViewModel and MainWindowViewModel directly with connection strings and Android services. TARGET: same AddMcpHost singleton bootstrap pattern, but with Android-specific adapter registrations (notifications, prefs, browser, wake-word/lifecycle integrations). Provider disposal is tied to Android app lifetime.

TD13 PHASE 2C MODIFY — src/McpServer.UI.Core/ViewModels/MainWindowViewModel.cs and src/McpServerManager.Core/ViewModels/MainWindowViewModel.cs. CURRENT UI.Core constructor block at L390-425 creates clients and services, then new UiCoreAppRuntime(...), then resolves Dispatcher. CURRENT Core wrapper constructor chain at L97-130 passes baseUrl/apiKey/bearerToken down. TARGET incremental migration: add a new DI-friendly constructor that accepts already-registered McpTodoService, McpWorkspaceService, McpVoiceConversationService, McpSessionLogService, McpAgentEventStreamService, WorkspaceContextViewModel, and Dispatcher from AddMcpHost connection-bootstrap mode. Preserve existing overloads as compatibility shims until all hosts migrate. Delete _uiCoreRuntime field/property and the new UiCoreAppRuntime block only after Desktop/Android stop using the legacy overloads.

TD14 PHASE 2D MODIFY — src/McpServerManager.Core/Services/ChatWindowViewModelFactory.cs L43-61 static CreateDispatcher: CURRENT builds ServiceCollection, registers chat services, AddCqrsDispatcher, AddCqrsHandlers(typeof(ChatOpenAgentConfigCommand).Assembly), then leaks the provider. TARGET: factory receives IServiceProvider, creates a scope per chat window, resolves Dispatcher from the scoped/root host graph, and keeps the scope alive with the window so disposal is explicit. Also register the chat handler assembly through AddMcpHost AdditionalHandlerAssemblies.

TD15 PHASE 2E DELETE — src/McpServerManager.Core/Services/LocalCqrsDispatcher.cs only after all direct consumers are migrated. Current consumers already include src/McpServerManager.Core/ViewModels/ConnectionViewModel.cs L22, src/McpServerManager.Core/ViewModels/ChatWindowViewModel.cs L35, src/McpServerManager.Core/ViewModels/LogViewModel.cs L23, src/McpServerManager.Core/ViewModels/SettingsViewModel.cs L41, and src/McpServerManager/ViewModels/ChatWindowViewModel.cs L14. The file cannot be deleted until each constructor accepts Dispatcher from DI or an explicit design-time fallback.

TD16 PHASE 3A — Logging refactor must fix the logger factories before removing post-build hooks. CURRENT blockers: src/McpServerManager.Core/Services/AppLogService.cs L45 and src/McpServer.UI.Core/Services/AppLogService.cs L45 implement AddProvider as a no-op, so AddCqrsLoggerProvider alone does nothing; src/McpServer.Director/DirectorLogging.cs L17-34 installs a prebuilt SerilogLoggerFactory instance, so DI-registered ILoggerProvider instances are not wired automatically. TARGET: (1) update both AppLogService implementations to retain added ILoggerProvider instances and return a composite logger that writes to AppLogService plus every attached provider logger; (2) introduce a DirectorCompositeLoggerFactory or equivalent wrapper in src/McpServer.Director/DirectorLogging.cs that delegates to Serilog but also honors AddProvider; (3) after those logger factories work, call AddCqrsLoggerProvider during service registration and delete the 3 post-build AddProvider hacks from UiCoreAppRuntime, UI.Core/UiCoreServiceProviderFactory, and DirectorServiceRegistration.

TD17 PHASE 3B — Web CQRS logging remains a separate path. src/McpServer.Web/WebServiceRegistration.cs correctly uses scoped Dispatcher because request services and bearer token access are scoped. Do not attempt to register Dispatcher as a singleton ILoggerProvider in Web. Add tests that verify DispatchLogRecord / RecentDispatches and any UI-facing log surfaces remain visible in scoped request contexts without introducing singleton/scoped DI cycles.

TD18 PHASE 4A NEW FILE — src/McpServer.UI.Core/Hosting/IMcpHostContext.cs: interface must match the current host shapes, so use read-oriented members plus operations rather than a settable ActiveWorkspacePath. Target shape: string? ActiveWorkspacePath { get; }, Task<McpServerClient> GetRequiredControlApiClientAsync(CancellationToken ct = default), Task<McpServerClient> GetRequiredActiveWorkspaceApiClientAsync(CancellationToken ct = default), bool TrySetActiveWorkspace(string? workspacePath), void RefreshBearerTokens(). Adapt existing implementations: src/McpServer.Director/DirectorMcpContext.cs already has equivalent control/workspace client accessors and refresh logic; src/McpServer.Web/WebMcpContext.cs already owns workspace sync and active/control clients but lives at src/McpServer.Web/WebMcpContext.cs (not under Services/). Desktop/Android need a new singleton AvaloniaMcpContext extracted from MainWindowViewModel state.

TD19 PHASE 4B MODIFY — src/McpServer.Director/DirectorServiceRegistration.cs Configure L31-86. CURRENT: manual logging, AddCqrs(typeof(Program).Assembly), AddUiCore, auth overrides, McpHttpClient creation, DirectorMcpContext registration, and 17 API client adapters. TARGET: delegate common graph creation to AddMcpHost (singleton, command-target mode) while preserving Director-specific auth overrides and DirectorMcpContext registration. BuildAndFinalize should collapse to a plain BuildServiceProvider only after TD16 logger-factory work lands.

TD20 PHASE 4C MODIFY — Consolidate 5 Director provider-entry sites, not 4: (1) src/McpServer.Director/Program.cs L47-50 BuildServiceProvider wrapper, (2) src/McpServer.Director/Commands/InteractiveCommand.cs L37-39, (3) src/McpServer.Director/Commands/CommandHelpers.cs L51-56, (4) src/McpServer.Director/Commands/AuthCommands.cs L75-79, (5) src/McpServer.Director/Commands/AgentHostCommand.cs L90-118. TARGET: central DirectorHost.CreateProvider(workspace, additionalConfig) factory used by Program, InteractiveCommand, CommandHelpers, AuthCommands, and AgentHostCommand so the composition root inventory is complete.

TD21 PHASE 4D MODIFY — src/McpServer.Web/WebServiceRegistration.cs L13-66 and src/McpServer.Web/WebMcpContext.cs. CURRENT: AddHttpContextAccessor, TryAddSingleton WebRoleContext, AddScoped BearerTokenAccessor, AddScoped<Dispatcher>, AddUiCore, scoped command-target chain, RemoveAll+AddScoped WorkspaceAutoSelector/BackendConnectionMonitor, scoped WebMcpContext, and scoped API adapters. TARGET: keep Web scoped but move the common graph to AddMcpHost(Lifetime=Scoped, command-target mode). Then layer the existing Web-specific overrides on top. File shrinks, but only after verifying WorkspaceContextViewModel and WebMcpContext behavior remain circuit-safe.

TD22 PHASE 5A NEW FILE — src/McpServer.UI.Core/Auth/IHostIdentityProvider.cs: public interface IHostIdentityProvider { string? GetBearerToken(); string? GetApiKey(); string? GetWorkspacePath(); }. Implementations: WebHostIdentityProvider wraps BearerTokenAccessor + current workspace context, DirectorHostIdentityProvider reads cached token state / active workspace from DirectorMcpContext, AvaloniaHostIdentityProvider reads the active connection model used by Desktop/Android. This phase standardizes identity; it should not be a prerequisite for the earlier connection-bootstrap work in TD6/TD8.

TD23 PHASE 5B MODIFY — src/McpServer.UI.Core/Hosting/McpHostOptions.cs adds optional IHostIdentityProvider or factory registration after TD22. This later phase replaces the interim host-specific credential lambdas/bootstrap fields introduced in TD6 with a unified abstraction. It should not remove the earlier bootstrap path until Desktop/Android and Web are already migrated and verified.

TD24 PHASE 6 CONVERGENCE TARGETS — After migration, each host composition file becomes a thin adapter, but the contract must support two shapes: command-target host shells (Desktop, Android, Web, Director) and service-provider-only hosts (VSIX Todo tool window). Size targets remain approximate: Desktop and Android reduce materially, WebServiceRegistration and DirectorServiceRegistration become declarative adapters, and VSIX shrinks without forcing an ICommandTarget implementation.

TD25 PHASE 7 FILES TO DELETE — 5 files, 305 total lines: (1) src/McpServerManager.Core/Services/UiCoreAppRuntime.cs, (2) src/McpServerManager.Core/Services/UiCoreServiceProviderFactory.cs, (3) src/McpServer.UI.Core/Services/UiCoreAppRuntime.cs, (4) src/McpServer.UI.Core/Services/UiCoreServiceProviderFactory.cs, (5) src/McpServerManager.Core/Services/LocalCqrsDispatcher.cs. Also remove the obsolete AddMcpServerManagerUiCore shim from src/McpServerManager.Core/ServiceCollectionExtensions.cs after all hosts are off it.

TD26 PHASE 7 VERIFICATION — 7 grep patterns, all must return 0 matches in src/ before closure: (1) grep -rn "new UiCoreAppRuntime" src/, (2) grep -rn "UiCoreServiceProviderFactory" src/, (3) grep -rn "GetRequiredService<ILoggerFactory>().AddProvider" src/, (4) grep -rn "LocalCqrsDispatcher" src/, (5) grep -rn "ChatWindowViewModelFactory.CreateDispatcher" src/, (6) grep -rn "AddMcpServerManagerUiCore" src/ (compat shim allowed only before Phase 7), (7) grep -rn "BuildAndFinalize\|BuildServiceProvider" src/McpServer.Director/ to confirm only the final consolidated Director provider factory remains.

implementationTasks.task: IT-01 Phase 0A: Validate the 12 provider-construction/composition sites in TD1, not 10. Run grep -rn "BuildServiceProvider\|BuildAndFinalize" src/ and explicitly include src/McpServer.Director/Program.cs L47-50 in the inventory because Program is a real Director provider-entry site. VERIFIED 2026-03-21: grep confirms the 12-site inventory and the 5 Director provider-entry sites listed in TD1/TD20.

IT-02 Phase 0B: Add pre-refactor DI smoke tests. Extend tests/McpServerManager.Core.Tests/Integration/DiContainerTests.cs to cover provider disposal and handler resolution; add scoped-host tests in tests/McpServer.Web.Tests/ for Dispatcher + WorkspaceContextViewModel + WebMcpContext; add logger-factory tests proving current AddProvider no-op behavior before the TD16 changes. VERIFIED 2026-03-21: focused Core and Web characterization slices pass from dedicated log captures (Core 15/15, Web 2/2).

IT-03 Phase 1A: Create src/McpServer.UI.Core/Hosting/McpHostLifetimeStrategy.cs per TD7 — public enum { Singleton, Scoped } and scoped IDispatcher registration helper for Web. VERIFIED 2026-03-21: shared helper now drives both singleton and scoped dispatcher registration paths; focused UI.Core, Web, and Core tests pass.

IT-04 Phase 1A: Create src/McpServer.UI.Core/Hosting/McpHostOptions.cs per TD6 with two activation modes: explicit service registrations and connection-bootstrap registrations. Keep CommandTarget optional so VSIX todo hosting can use AddMcpHost without implementing ICommandTarget. VERIFIED 2026-03-21: shared options contract now captures lifetime, optional command target, explicit API clients, connection-bootstrap fields, and host infrastructure overrides.

IT-05 Phase 1A: Create src/McpServer.UI.Core/Hosting/McpHostBuilderExtensions.cs per TD8 — AddMcpHost(Action<McpHostOptions>) extension. Support optional command-target registration, AddUiCore composition, connection-bootstrap creation of Mcp services for Avalonia, and defer AddCqrsLoggerProvider until TD16 logger-factory work is complete. VERIFIED 2026-03-21: shared builder composes explicit-client and connection-bootstrap modes, lifetime-aware dispatcher registration, optional command-target aliases, and adapter-backed MCP services; focused UI.Core hosting tests pass.

IT-06 Phase 1B: Convert src/McpServerManager.Core/ServiceCollectionExtensions.cs L43-130 AddMcpServerManagerUiCore into an [Obsolete] shim per TD10. Delegate to AddMcpHost with Avalonia adapters and existing signature compatibility. VERIFIED 2026-03-21: the legacy extension now delegates shared registrations to AddMcpHost, preserves Core logger plus command-target aliases, and focused DiContainerTests/CommandRoundTripTests pass.

IT-07 Phase 2A: Migrate src/McpServerManager.Desktop/App.axaml.cs per TD11 — replace manual ConnectionViewModel/MainWindowViewModel construction with DI resolution from a root provider built once for the app lifetime using AddMcpHost(connection-bootstrap mode, singleton lifetime). COMPLETED 2026-03-21: Desktop now resolves ConnectionViewModel from an app-lifetime provider and creates MainWindowViewModel from a per-connection AddMcpHost-backed session provider using provider-backed host services plus deferred command-target attachment, with Desktop build and Desktop headless tests passing.

IT-08 Phase 2B: Migrate src/McpServerManager.Android/App.axaml.cs per TD12 using the same AddMcpHost bootstrap pattern as Desktop while preserving Android lifecycle and SharedPreferences connection behavior. COMPLETED 2026-03-21: Android now resolves ConnectionViewModel from an app-lifetime provider and creates MainWindowViewModel from a per-connection AddMcpHost-backed session provider while preserving Android browser/QR/OIDC hooks, logout flow, and SharedPreferences-backed connection/workspace persistence; Android compile target succeeds.

IT-09 Phase 2C: Refactor src/McpServer.UI.Core/ViewModels/MainWindowViewModel.cs and src/McpServerManager.Core/ViewModels/MainWindowViewModel.cs per TD13. Add a new DI-friendly constructor that takes registered MCP services + Dispatcher + WorkspaceContextViewModel, keep legacy overloads as shims initially, and remove _uiCoreRuntime only after hosts stop calling legacy constructors. COMPLETED 2026-03-21: UI.Core legacy construction now shims through the same MainWindowHostServices-based initialization path used by migrated hosts, while Core preserves both constructor paths; legacy app build, Desktop build/tests, and Android compile all pass.

IT-10 Phase 2D: Fix src/McpServerManager.Core/Services/ChatWindowViewModelFactory.cs per TD14 — inject IServiceProvider, use a scope-per-window instead of static CreateDispatcher(), and register chat handler assemblies through AddMcpHost AdditionalHandlerAssemblies. COMPLETED 2026-03-21: ChatWindowViewModelFactory now creates a DI scope per chat window, Desktop resolves the factory from the host session provider, Desktop chat-window lifetime disposes the scope on close, and source grep confirms the old CreateDispatcher/new-factory patterns are gone.

IT-11 Phase 2E: Remove LocalCqrsDispatcher only after migrating every direct caller named in TD15, including ConnectionViewModel, Core ChatWindowViewModel, desktop-shell ChatWindowViewModel, LogViewModel, and SettingsViewModel. Delete src/McpServerManager.Core/Services/LocalCqrsDispatcher.cs only when grep -rn "LocalCqrsDispatcher" src/ returns 0. COMPLETED 2026-03-21: host-owned dispatcher paths now cover ConnectionViewModel, LogViewModel, SettingsViewModel, and managed Desktop chat; the remaining chat fallbacks were moved to a dedicated chat dispatcher bootstrap, LocalCqrsDispatcher.cs was deleted, legacy shell/Desktop/Android validations passed, and src/ grep for LocalCqrsDispatcher returns zero matches.

IT-12 Phase 3A: Implement TD16 before deleting any post-build hooks. Modify src/McpServerManager.Core/Services/AppLogService.cs and src/McpServer.UI.Core/Services/AppLogService.cs so AddProvider stores providers and composite loggers fan out to them; modify src/McpServer.Director/DirectorLogging.cs so the logger factory also honors AddProvider. Only then add AddCqrsLoggerProvider in registration and delete the 3 explicit AddProvider post-build calls.

IT-13 Phase 3B: Add Web logging tests per TD17. Verify scoped Dispatcher logging surfaces remain visible without registering Dispatcher as a singleton ILoggerProvider. No production change in Web should attempt to reintroduce that cycle.

IT-14 Phase 4A: Create src/McpServer.UI.Core/Hosting/IMcpHostContext.cs per TD18 with method-based workspace switching and async client accessors. Update src/McpServer.Director/DirectorMcpContext.cs and src/McpServer.Web/WebMcpContext.cs to implement it, then extract an AvaloniaMcpContext from Desktop/Android state.

IT-15 Phase 4B: Refactor src/McpServer.Director/DirectorServiceRegistration.cs per TD19 so common graph creation goes through AddMcpHost and Director-specific auth/context adapters remain layered on top. Do not simplify BuildAndFinalize until the TD16 logger-factory work is already in place.

IT-16 Phase 4C: Consolidate all 5 Director provider-entry sites listed in TD20 — Program, InteractiveCommand, CommandHelpers, AuthCommands, and AgentHostCommand — behind one DirectorHost.CreateProvider(workspace, additionalConfig) factory.

IT-17 Phase 4D: Refactor src/McpServer.Web/WebServiceRegistration.cs and src/McpServer.Web/WebMcpContext.cs per TD21 — move common registration to AddMcpHost(scoped, command-target mode) while keeping Web-specific scoped overrides, circuit-safe workspace behavior, and bearer-token forwarding.

IT-18 Phase 5A: Create src/McpServer.UI.Core/Auth/IHostIdentityProvider.cs per TD22 and implement Web, Director, and Avalonia variants. This phase standardizes identity after the earlier connection-bootstrap path is already working.

IT-19 Phase 5B: Replace the interim bootstrap credential fields in McpHostOptions with IHostIdentityProvider where possible, but only after all migrated hosts pass regression tests for JWT precedence, API-key fallback, and workspace resolution semantics.

IT-20 Phase 6: Converge host composition roots to thin adapters per TD24, preserving both command-target hosts and service-provider-only hosts. Verify Desktop, Android, Web, Director, and VSIX each still compile and run with their correct host shape.

IT-21 Phase 6D: Migrate src/McpServer.VsExtension.McpTodo.Vsix/McpServerMcpTodoToolWindowPane.cs per TD24 using AddMcpHost in non-command-target mode. Preserve the existing TodoListHostViewModel pattern that resolves Dispatcher from IServiceProvider and keep Dispose(bool) ownership intact.
IT-22 Phase 7A: Delete the 5 obsolete files in TD25 and remove the AddMcpServerManagerUiCore shim once all host code paths are off it and the LocalCqrsDispatcher grep is clean.

IT-23 Phase 7B: Update docs/architecture/DI-Deviation-Matrix.md and DI-Deviation-Diagram.md to match the corrected steady state, including the two host modes and the completed logger-factory refactor.

IT-24 Phase 7C: Final verification — run the 7 greps in TD26, build all affected projects, and run the DI/lifetime/logging test suites. Do not close the todo until Director Program is included in the consolidation verification and no legacy logger hook remains.

remaining: Current focus: IT-12 logger-factory refactor so AddProvider actually fans out to registered providers and the post-build dispatcher AddProvider calls can be removed.
functionalRequirements: FR1. All five hosts must continue to expose the same current end-user capabilities after the refactor: session browsing, workspace switching, todo management, voice interactions, preview/navigation actions, and configuration actions.
FR2. Web must preserve per-request/per-user isolation so no scoped state, bearer token, or workspace context can bleed between users.
FR3. Desktop and Android must preserve current single-user behavior, startup experience, saved connection behavior, clipboard interactions, notifications, and UI-thread dispatch behavior.
FR4. Director must preserve CLI/TUI workspace switching semantics and role-based authorization behavior driven by DirectorRoleContext and DirectorAuthorizationPolicyService.
FR5. CQRS-driven UI logging/status behavior must be available consistently across hosts; restoring Web parity is part of the remediation, not optional cleanup.
FR6. The shared registration path must remain extensible so future hosts can inject host-only adapters without forking the base graph.
FR7. VS Extension must continue to function as a self-contained tool window with proper ServiceProvider disposal, todo editing, and workspace-path resolution from the active solution.

FR8. Director commands that currently build isolated service providers (InteractiveCommand, AuthCommands, AgentHostCommand) must retain equivalent isolation semantics after consolidation - a failed command must not corrupt the parent session state.
technicalRequirements: TR1. Introduce one canonical registration API in shared code that separates: base service registrations, host adapter registrations, lifetime strategy (singleton vs scoped), and post-build hooks.

TR2. Eliminate the need for host-specific manual BuildServiceProvider patterns inside UiCoreAppRuntime wrappers. Host code should rely on IServiceCollection plus Generic Host or ASP.NET Core builder where available.

TR3. Remove the Dispatcher/ILoggerFactory circular-dependency workaround (present in 4 files: UiCoreServiceProviderFactory.cs:49, UiCoreAppRuntime.cs:47, UI.Core/UiCoreServiceProviderFactory.cs:83, DirectorServiceRegistration.cs:94) by introducing an explicit CQRS event/log sink or deferred provider mechanism that works in singleton and scoped environments.
TR4. Make McpClient, WorkspaceContextViewModel, and API adapter registrations lifetime-safe by design. Scoped hosts must resolve scoped instances; singleton hosts must not require fake scopes.

TR5. Replace direct auth-shape knowledge in shared registrations with a host-facing identity abstraction that can represent bearer-token, API-key, cached-token, or file-backed role contexts. Preserve the existing TryAdd-first-wins override pattern for IRoleContext and IAuthorizationPolicyService.
TR6. The migration must land incrementally so each host can compile and run after each phase; avoid a flag day rewrite.
TR7. Preserve existing public APIs where practical. If signatures change, provide temporary compatibility shims until all hosts are migrated.

TR8. Add or update automated verification for service registration correctness, dispatcher logging behavior, and host-specific lifetime boundaries. Build on the existing test infrastructure in DiContainerTests.cs, ServiceCollectionExtensionsTests.cs, and UiCoreTestHost.cs.

TR9. All manually-created ServiceProvider instances must have a clear ownership and disposal path. Eliminate provider leaks in ChatWindowViewModelFactory, migrate LocalCqrsDispatcher static singleton to host-owned DI, and ensure Desktop/Android providers are disposed on app shutdown.

TR10. Consolidate Director command-level container construction (4+ separate BuildServiceProvider sites across InteractiveCommand, CommandHelpers, AuthCommands, AgentHostCommand) into a shared composition entry point that supports command-scoped lifetime without rebuilding the full container each time.

TR11. The shared composition contract must support both singleton Dispatcher (Desktop/Android/Director) and scoped Dispatcher (Web per-request) without requiring host-specific conditional registration logic in the shared code.
